title: Split How much character development does Rei really get
display_title: Split How much character development does Rei really get
wikitext:
[]: [Split] How much character development does Rei really get?             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Sep 11, 2015 7:39 am Split after this post in the How Seriously Do You Take Shipping? thread: http://forum.evageeks.org/post/809458/How-seriously-do-you-take-shipping/#809458 Alaska Slim wrote: She's the only character whose development arc did not stall; it was instead tragically cut short by circumstance.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Sep 11, 2015 8:23 am You'll need to watch the newer movies to find some Rei II that doesn't spend all of her time being forgotten.

[]: Re: Rei grew, to say little. Bl             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Fri Sep 11, 2015 3:57 pm Bagheera wrote: Dunno what show you were watching,

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Sep 11, 2015 4:43 pm Don't forget the conversation with Toji on the school roof. Brief, but there's substance there.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Sep 11, 2015 5:00 pm NemZ wrote: Don't forget the conversation with Toji on the school roof. Brief, but there's substance there.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Sep 11, 2015 5:17 pm Well, contrary to what Toji assumes there I fully expect she was there to talk to him. That means Rei took note of his behavior being different and felt some compulsion to seek him out even without having some reason in particular to care. That's a big step. Toji is certainly correct about her having a thing for Shinji, but this shows she's not just fixated on an Ikari but learning to branch out a bit and feel empathy for others. Reaching out to Asuka in 22 couldn't possibly have happened without this intermediate step. And I believe my common complaint is that after 6 Asuka derails the lovely little Shinji/Rei story and steals the spotlight, not that absolutely nothing happens to Rei as a character afterwards.

[]: Rei is not a hedgehog. BP             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Fri Sep 11, 2015 11:44 pm Bagheera wrote: Rei's "development" is 1-6, 23, EoE. What little we get in-between is just a reiteration of what came before.

[]:             Quote Post by unz » Sat Sep 12, 2015 12:26 pm What Alaska Slim said, other than interior monologues and reactions Rei growth is delivered through details of relationship. I should go through episodes again but you can see how she ignores people less and less.

[]:             Quote Post by Compiling_Autumn » Sat Sep 12, 2015 6:47 pm So, the point? Rei manages to break her own filtration of reality before everyone else, rejecting the hedgehog's dilemma, which is part to why she could serve as a guide for others during instrumentality. "You can only show other people what reality is after you've seen it for yourself", or something like that. Rei is ahead of the pack, and was perhaps from the start able to perceive reality better than anyone.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Sep 12, 2015 7:58 pm I know I usually like to elaborate on things like context and thematic consistency when discussing character development, but it really isn't needed when answering "How much character development does Rei really get?" This question deals with quantity, not quality. To answer the question of quantity, the answer is simple: Ep 1 (Foreshadowing) Ep 5-6 (Proper Introduction; Smiling) Ep 11 (More Foreshadowing) Ep 12 (Doesn't like meat) Ep 19 (Conflict with Shinji) Ep 20 (Inviting Becoming One) Ep 21 (Child Rei; First Death and Rebirth) Ep 22 (Awkward Conversation about not being a doll) Ep 23 (Rejecting Becoming One; Second Death and Rebirth) Ep 24 (Same as Kaworu; Massive Off-Screen AT Field Battle with Tabris) Ep 25 (Reconciliation of Self) Ep 26 (Congratulations, Shinji!) EoE (I Am Not a Doll!)

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Sep 12, 2015 8:40 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: Ep 19 (Conflict with Shinji)

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Sep 12, 2015 8:46 pm you're skipping a lot there, F4. I don't have my copy available at present but making a few additons just off the top of my head: Ep 1 (Foreshadowing) Ep 3 (Mysterious cold presence) Ep 5-6 (Proper Introduction; Smiling) Ep 9 (If I'm ordered to, I will.) Ep 10 (Shocked she's not the one chosen for high risk duty) Ep 11 (More Foreshadowing; first conflict with Asuka ) Ep 12 (Doesn't like meat) Ep 14 (Rei's poem) Ep 16 (Do you only pilot eva for the praise of others?) Ep 17 (talking with Toji on the roof) Ep 18 (hesitates to take the shot) Ep 19 (Conflict with Shinji to what are you referring? ; Dreams? ) Ep 20 ([s]Inviting Becoming One[/s] that's Yui, not her ; Well, that's good for you ) Ep 21 (Child Rei; First Death and Rebirth) Ep 22 (Awkward Conversation about not being a doll) Ep 23 (Rejecting Becoming One; Second Death and Rebirth) Ep 24 (Same as Kaworu; Massive Off-Screen AT Field Battle with Tabris) Ep 25 (Reconciliation of Self) Ep 26 (Congratulations, Shinji!) EoE (I Am Not a Doll!)

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Sep 12, 2015 9:12 pm Sachi wrote: What conflict?

[]: Rei FTW! BP             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Sat Sep 12, 2015 9:23 pm Compiling_Autumn wrote: At the risk of sounding cruel, bonding with others isn't as simple as that. There are people who get along famously with others, who inspire trust and devotion from friends and lovers, and then there are people like Rei who end up, in spite of their good intentions, being mostly alone due to poor circumstances and a lack of assertiveness.

[]: Re: Rei FTW! BP             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Sep 12, 2015 9:38 pm Alaska Slim wrote: More than just Foreshadowing occurs here, stating that she's "not the favorite" is a subtle point on the relationship she has with Gendo. Something that is perhaps best on the display when Gendo bears his heart in EoE. He doesn't apologize to her . Rather, he apologizes to Shinji.

[]: Re: Rei FTW! BP             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Sat Sep 12, 2015 11:12 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: That's kinda what I meant by foreshadowing. But I do also consider it character development, which it why I listed it in the first place.

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Sat Sep 12, 2015 11:49 pm I think there's a lot of things Rei is saying with that statement. One could even interpret it as being dismissive of man's futile attempt to fight against the things he ultimately cannot defeat.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Sep 13, 2015 3:19 am FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: The train scene. She's, again, asking Shinji if he's ever tried to understand his father.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Sep 14, 2015 7:42 am Sachi wrote: If it's all in Shinji's head, I don't think it counts.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Sep 14, 2015 10:29 am The keyword in my statement was "arguably." :P It could also be Quantum!Rei looking back on things from EoE. That scene has always been a bit controversial because of Toji's involvement and there's never been solid explanations either way, so I'm hesitant to jump to conclusions such as Rei communicating through dreams. We can leave it at arguable.
summary:
[]: [Split] How much character development does Rei really get?             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Sep 11, 2015 7:39 am Split after this post in the How Seriously Do You Take Shipping? thread: http://forum.evageeks.org/post/809458/How-seriously-do-you-take-shipping/#809458 Alaska Slim wrote: She's the only character whose development arc did not stall; it was instead tragically cut short by circumstance.

[]:    &nb
